description: PRAGMA synchronous=OFF disables fsync() calls on SQLite. While this improves write performance, any OS crash or power failure between a write and the next checkpoint will corrupt the WAL file and potentially the main database. For financial data (DFP/ITR reports), this is a durability risk. WAL mode without fsync offers virtually no crash recovery guarantee.
recommendation: Use `PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL` (default for WAL mode), which maintains good performance while ensuring atomic commits survive OS crashes. Only use `OFF` if the data is entirely reproducible from source.
fix_example: pragma_conn.execute(text("PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL"))  # safe for WAL

code_snippet:
placeholders = ', '.join(str(y) for y in sorted(years_to_delete))
f'..."REPORT_YEAR" IN ({placeholders})...'
description: Year values are interpolated directly into a SQL DELETE via f-string. Currently safe because years are integers derived from column name parsing, but this bypasses SQLAlchemy's parameterization layer. Also flagged as CORR-004 — the security concern here is about the pattern itself being a footgun for future maintainers.
recommendation: Use expanding bindparam to keep years parameterized. See CORR-004 for fix example.
fix_example: # Same as CORR-004 fix — use bindparam('years', expanding=True)

description: Exception messages from file loading (including full file paths) are printed to stdout. In a production context (e.g., Streamlit Cloud logs, CI/CD), exception details may expose internal paths. Low risk here but inconsistent with database.py which uses logger.warning().
recommendation: Replace print() with logger.warning() throughout scraper.py for consistent structured logging.
fix_example:
import logging
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
# ...
logger.warning('Failed to load standardizer: %s', e)

code_snippet: SELECT DISTINCT CD_CVM, COMPANY_NAME FROM financial_reports WHERE COMPANY_NAME IS NOT NULL
description: All SQL queries in loaders.py use SQLAlchemy text() with named :param bindings or bindparam(expanding=True). No string concatenation into SQL was found. The codebase is clean on SQL injection for the dashboard layer.
recommendation: No action required. Document this in CLAUDE.md as a confirmed safe pattern.
